meta.method: X profiles checked live in browser (top visible post = last_seen lower bound). Newsletters and blogs checked via web search against archives. See SIGNALS.md.
meta.curator: Hand-curated by Florian only. The agent never edits this file. whitelist: yes entries (verified_active channels only) are the signal-tier sourcing whitelist.

people.name: Peter Beck
people.why: Posts genuine Electron and Neutron engineering detail and answers technical replies himself.

people.name: Tory Bruno
people.role: Former CEO of ULA; reported to have joined Blue Origin
people.why: Rare executive candor on propulsion and vehicle design trade-offs, explains rocket engineering in reply threads.
people.notes: Role change caught 2026-07-05: MECO Feb 2026 show notes cite Ars Technica reporting Bruno out as ULA CEO and joining Blue Origin. His Jul 1 repost of Blue Origin launch-ops content is consistent. Confirm role before publishing a profile.

channels.notes: Identity: Bio self-states 'President and CEO United Launch Alliance @ULAlaunch' (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=torybruno.bsky.social), matching known org (ULA) directly.

people.name: Will Marshall
people.why: First-hand Planet strategy framing, Pelican and edge-AI milestones.
people.notes: Handle corrected 2026-07-05: @wsm1 is dead, current handle is @Will4Planet. Review flag is editorial, not technical: he is the operator's own CEO; decide whether MCC covering him creates awkwardness.

people.name: Josef Aschbacher
people.why: Primary source for European program decisions, budgets, and Ariane and Copernicus milestones.

channels.notes: Identity: Verified custom-domain handle on esa.int (ESA's own domain, DNS-verified by Bluesky) plus bio 'The Director General of the European Space Agency' (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=josefaschbacher.esa.int).

people.name: Max Haot
people.why: Posts Haven-1 hardware and test progress directly; one of the few commercial station programs with real build photos.

people.name: Jared Isaacman
people.why: Runs NASA and still posts personally; Polaris background gives him unusual first-hand credibility on commercial crew.
people.notes: Status change caught 2026-07-05: no longer just Polaris commander/nominee, he is Administrator and posts officially as @NASAAdmin. Content mix on the personal account will shift toward official messaging.

channels.notes: Official account, seen via repost; not directly checked.

people.name: Pawan Kumar Chandana
people.why: First-hand Vikram-1 test and integration updates; core voice of Indian private launch.
people.notes: Handle existence verified via search 2026-07-05, but recent-activity sampling not done (search snippets showed 2023-2024 posts). Re-check before whitelisting.

people.name: Joe Morrison
people.why: Brutally candid takes on EO business models and pricing that no other operator executive will say publicly.
people.notes: X active; his Substack (A Closer Look) is dormant since roughly 2023 but the archive is canonical for EO business-model critique.

channels.notes: Dormant; keep for archive links.

Identity: Bio self-states 'VP of Commercial Experience at Umbra Space' (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=joemorrison.bsky.social), matching known org (Umbra). Account is dormant (last post 2023-06-15) but identity is ce

people.name: Elon Musk
people.why: Primary source for Starship and Starlink, buried in a very low signal-to-noise feed.
people.ingest_rules: If whitelisted, ingest only posts matching SpaceX, Starship, Starlink, Falcon, Raptor, Dragon keywords.
people.notes: Not re-verified; existence and activity not in question.

people.name: Jonathan McDowell
people.why: The canonical independent orbital catalog: launch logs, Starlink shell statistics, reentry analysis nobody else produces.
people.notes: Shifting activity toward Bluesky; ingest both channels.

channels.notes: JSR newsletter since 1989; annual space activities report published Feb 2026.

people.name: Marco Langbroek
people.why: Optically tracks classified payloads and reconstructs orbits of secretive missions.

channels.notes: Blog URL from memory; confirm during RSS discovery.

people.name: Scott Tilley
people.why: Detects anomalous satellite behavior and zombie satellites from RF signal analysis; a genuinely unique data source.

people.name: Blaine Curcio
people.role: Founder, Orbital Gateway Consulting; publisher, China Space Monitor
people.why: The leading English-language analyst of Chinese commercial space (Guowang, Qianfan, commercial launchers); testified to USCC.
people.notes: Handle corrected 2026-07-05: @BlaineCurcio does not exist, current handle is @ChinaSpaceGuy.

channels.notes: Monthly cadence per publication; ~2.6K subs; latest issue date not sampled.

people.name: Caleb Henry
people.why: Deep satcom constellation and manufacturing analysis with a finance lens; ex-SpaceNews.
people.notes: Handle found 2026-07-05: @ChenrySpace (the guessed @CHenry_QA does not exist).

channels.notes: Identity: Bio self-states 'Director of Research at Quilty Space' and links https://chenryspace.substack.com/ (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=chenryspace.bsky.social); handle also matches known X handle ChenrySpace.

people.name: Tim Farrar
people.why: Contrarian, numbers-driven analysis of Starlink and Kuiper economics.

channels.notes: Identity: Bio self-states 'Consultant in satellite communications and wireless spectrum' (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=tmfassociates.bsky.social); handle matches known X handle TMFAssociates.

people.name: Pierre Lionnet
people.why: Publicly dismantles inflated space-economy market figures; the sharpest European skeptic.
people.notes: Handle confirmed 2026-07-05 (@LionnetPierre, posted within 24h). LinkedIn remains his higher-volume channel.

channels.notes: Identity: Bio self-states 'Research and Managing Director at Eurospace' (checked via app.bsky.actor.getProfile?actor=lionnetpierre.bsky.social), matching known org exactly. Identity is certain; note account has 0 posts (postsCount

people.name: Mo Islam
people.why: Space-finance and dealflow commentary with an investor lens; Payload's research arm publishes real numbers.

people.name: Brian Weeden
people.why: Authoritative on regulatory and debris policy, posts with primary documents attached.

people.name: Delian Asparouhov
people.why: High-signal on space venture financing from inside the deals.
people.notes: High posting volume with a large off-topic share; whitelist only with a keyword filter, or keep public-page only.

people.name: Laura Seward Forczyk
people.why: Space workforce and commercial human-spaceflight market analysis.
people.notes: Latest visible activity in the 2026-07-05 sample was a May 15 repost; low cadence.

people.name: Aravind Ravichandran
people.why: The EO-market must-read: sensor economics, adoption barriers, use-case analysis.
people.notes: No working X handle (@aravind_raves is dead as of 2026-07-05). Newsletter and LinkedIn are his channels; whitelisting requires newsletter ingestion to be built.

channels.notes: Newsletter confirmed live; per-issue dates not sampled.

people.name: Ian Vorbach
people.why: Investor-lens essays and founder interviews (Capella's Banazadeh, Umbra's Dominocielo, Lori Garver).
people.notes: Publication migrated to beehiiv and added contributing writers; recent cadence unconfirmed as of 2026-07-05 (latest post found in search sample: Feb 2024). Check archive before whitelisting.

people.name: Erik Kulu
people.why: Maintains constellation, smallsat, and funding databases with annual reviews; more useful for the Registry surface than Signals.
people.notes: Medium confidence current; verify update recency during Registry work.

people.name: Casey Handmer
people.why: Irregular but seismic essays (e.g. 'Starship is still a big deal') that circulate through every space company.
people.notes: Conflict of interest: writes as a founder with positions, not a neutral analyst. High confidence active, not re-verified 2026-07-05. Irregular cadence.

channels.notes: Identity: Bio (via getProfile) states 'Founder Terraform Industries', matching known org exactly.

people.name: Casey Dreier
people.why: The best NASA budget analysis anywhere; org-affiliated but rigorous.
people.notes: Active (guest on MECO discussing the FY2026 budget, spring 2026). X handle not verified in this run; add during next verification pass.

channels.notes: Identity: Bio states 'Chief of Space Policy at The Planetary Society. Host of Planetary Radio: Space Policy Edition.' and links planetary.org, matching known org and site exactly.

people.name: Anthony Colangelo
people.why: Independent opinion and analysis since 2016, reader-funded; the closest structural analog to Daring Fireball in this industry.

channels.notes: Identity: mainenginecutoff.com (his known site) links directly to https://acolangelo.com (href="https://acolangelo.com"), which is his verified custom-domain Bluesky handle.

people.name: Eric Berger
people.why: The best sourcing inside SpaceX and NASA; routinely breaks schedule and program news.
people.notes: Profile served only his pinned post during the 2026-07-05 check, so no last_seen timestamp; activity is not in genuine doubt.

channels.notes: Identity: X bio (via api.fxtwitter.com/SciGuySpace, verified account) reads 'Senior Space Editor at Ars Technica. Likes rockets.'; Bluesky bio is near-identical distinctive text 'Senior Space Editor, Ars Technica. Likes rockets.'

people.name: Jeff Foust

people.why: Near-real-time coverage of hearings, conferences, and policy with high accuracy.

channels.notes: Identity: X bio (api.fxtwitter.com/jeff_foust) explicitly states 'Also @jfoust.bsky.social.' — direct link from his known X account.

people.name: Sandra Erwin
people.why: The main public source on Space Force procurement and commercial imagery contracts.

people.name: Michael Sheetz

channels.notes: Identity: Bio explicitly states 'f/k/@ thesheetztweetz' — his exact known X handle, self-declared as former handle. Also confirms job change: 'Firefly Director of Investor Relations | previously @CNBC'.

people.name: Joey Roulette
people.why: Strong scoops on NASA politics and SpaceX incidents.

channels.notes: Identity: Handle is an exact match to his known X handle joroulette; bio 'Reuters' space reporter' matches known org exactly (X bio also confirms Reuters via website field reuters.com/authors/joey-roulette).

people.name: Stephen Clark
people.why: The most complete launch-by-launch reporting including non-US vehicles.

outlets.name: Payload

outlets.notes: Media company, not whitelist-eligible under the named-individual rule; track as a source outlet.

outlets.name: The Orbital Index

outlets.why: Weekly contributed long-form essays since 2003, edited by Jeff Foust.
